summary: Matched `src/melee/vi/vi1101.c`, especially `un_8031F714_OnEnter`, and flipped `melee/vi/vi1101.c` from NonMatching to Matching in `configure.py`. The PR also corrected vi1101 data/sdata/sdata2 symbol layout, replaced placeholder/header declarations with concrete types, moved `HSD_MtxColMagFloat` out of `mtx.h` into C files needing it, and added targeted `MSL/math_ppc.h` includes. decomp.dev reported 12 new matches, including `main/melee/vi/vi1101` `un_8031F714_OnEnter` +588 bytes and vi1101 `.data`, `.sdata`, `.sdata2`, and `.sbss` reaching 100%. No human review feedback was present in the slice.

key_files.reason: Main matching work: added concrete data definitions, constants, archive-loading scene setup, and matched `un_8031F714_OnEnter`.
Removed placeholder usage and tightened prototypes/externs for vi1101 symbols.
Updated vi1101 object sizes/scopes and converted several sdata2 float symbols to local labels.
Removed `MSL/math_ppc.h` and the static inline `HSD_MtxColMagFloat` from the header.
Added `MSL/math_ppc.h` and a local inline definition of `HSD_MtxColMagFloat`.
Added `MSL/math_ppc.h` and a local inline definition of `HSD_MtxColMagFloat`.
Mostly formatting/signature wrapping changes adjacent to matching-sensitive code; no strong evidence of semantic changes.
Mostly formatting of casts/calls; likely collateral from include/style cleanup.

what_changed: Implemented `un_8031F714_OnEnter(void* arg)` in `vi1101.c` instead of leaving it as a nonmatching placeholder comment.
Defined a `Vi1101Data` struct containing three spawn `Vec3`s and resource-name strings: `"Vi1101.dat"`, `"visual1101Scene"`, and `"visual1101Cam2Scene"`.
Changed vi1101 spawn access from a bare `Vec3 un_80400200[3]` to `un_80400200.spawns`, reflecting that the data symbol also contains following strings.
Added concrete small-data constants in `vi1101.c`, including `GXColor un_804D5B08 = { 0, 0, 0, 0xff }` and local float constants for `1.0f`, `0.0f`, and `432.0f`.
Adjusted `un_8031F294` arguments from named `int char_index, costume_index` to `s32 arg0, arg1`, used `VecMtxPtr`, `GET_JOBJ`, and constant symbols to satisfy matching.
Changed `fn_8031F56C` signature from one argument to `(HSD_GObj* gobj, int unused)`, matching the callback shape used by `GObj_SetupGXLinkMax`.
Updated archive loading in `un_8031F714_OnEnter` to derive symbol names by offsets into the packed `un_80400200` data block and to load character animation via `viGetCharAnimByIndex`.

Updated `symbols.txt`: shrank `un_80400200` from size `0x58` to `0x54`, added local label `...data.0`, shrank `un_804D5B08` from `0x8` to `0x4`, and converted several vi1101 sdata2 globals to local `@166`-style float labels.
Moved `HSD_MtxColMagFloat` out of `src/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.h` and into C files that need it, while adding explicit `MSL/math_ppc.h` includes to multiple affected translation units.
Flipped vi1101 to Matching in the build configuration.
smart_moves: Recognized that `un_80400200` was not only a spawn array but a packed data object containing both `Vec3` spawn positions and resource strings; modeling it as `Vi1101Data` fixed the `.data` layout.
Used local static float constants for repeated literals (`1.0f`, `0.0f`, `432.0f`) to align `.sdata2` symbol ownership with the compiler output.
Matched the audio bitmask expression as `((u64) 0x20 << 48) | lbAudioAx_80026E84(arg0)`, preserving the intended 64-bit constant construction.
Kept callback signatures honest by adding the unused second parameter for the camera GX-link callback instead of forcing an incompatible one-argument function.
Reduced header-side inline/include pollution by removing `HSD_MtxColMagFloat` from `mtx.h` and adding `math_ppc.h` only to source files that require `sqrtf` behavior.
Updated the symbol map in parallel with source changes, preventing data-size and scope mismatches from masking source-level matching progress.
decomp_lessons: When a data symbol appears larger than the visible array, check whether adjacent strings or descriptors belong to the same compiled object before splitting it into separate globals.
For matching data sections, structuring packed constants and strings can be as important as matching function control flow.
Small-data float literals may need to be represented as local constants or local symbol-map labels rather than exported `un_` globals.
Callback functions must match the arity expected by the registration API; unused parameters can be required for codegen or ABI shape.
Moving static inline math helpers out of shared headers can avoid unintended inline/codegen effects across translation units.
A PR can mark an object Matching only after source, symbol sizes, local/global scopes, and configure state are all consistent.
naming_conventions: `un_8031F714_OnEnter` follows the scene lifecycle naming pattern with address prefix plus `_OnEnter` suffix.
`un_8031F960_OnFrame` remains the vi1101 frame callback naming pattern.
Unknown globals retain `un_` address-based names, e.g. `un_80400200`, `un_804D6FC0`, `un_804D6FC8`, until better semantic names are known.
Temporary struct fields remain conservative as `unk0` and `unk4` when evidence for semantic names is weak.
Local sdata2 constants in `symbols.txt` use compiler-style `@166`, `@167`, etc. labels rather than global `un_` names.
Function headers use address comments such as `/* 31F294 */` for exported vi1101 functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Pack related `.data` into a C struct to reproduce object size and address layout.
Use `char* data = (char*) &un_80400200` plus fixed offsets to reference embedded archive symbol strings, matching the original pointer arithmetic.
Use `VecMtxPtr` and assign `pmtx[1]`/`pmtx[2]` from spawn entries to align matrix/vector access codegen.
Use `GET_JOBJ(gobj)`/`GET_JOBJ(un_804D6FD0.unk0)` instead of raw `gobj->hsd_obj` casts where that better matches existing macros/codegen.
Represent literal animation frames and facing directions through local constants to control `.sdata2` references.
Place `HSD_MtxColMagFloat` as a non-header inline in the C files that need it to preserve per-translation-unit inlining behavior.
Add explicit `MSL/math_ppc.h` includes in source files using PPC math intrinsics or `sqrtf`-related inlines after removing the header-level include.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code increased by 588 bytes and linked code by 1772 bytes for GALE01.
Reported 12 new matches, including `main/melee/vi/vi1101` `un_8031F714_OnEnter` from 0.00% to 100.00%.
No human review comments or explicit reviewer rationale were present.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `Vi1101Data` fields or replace `unk0`/`unk4` with semantic names.
Search for other `vi` scene files with packed data objects containing spawn arrays plus archive symbol strings.
Check whether `HSD_MtxColMagFloat` duplication in `displayfunc.c` and `robj.c` was later consolidated or renamed.
Look for PRs that convert remaining `melee/vi/vi1201v1.c`, `vi1201v2.c`, or `vi1202.c` from NonMatching to Matching using similar scene-entry tactics.
Audit symbol-map local/global scope changes around `.sdata2` floats for other scene files.
